ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general green tea beverage class; no specific lemon green tea with no topping entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EJX covers ready-to-drink green tea; lemon flavouring and no-topping variant not separately coded. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for lemon green tea beverage without topping; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

preparation_method: lemon juice or lemon slices added

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of lemon green tea (no topping) is approximately 250 ml (250 g), equivalent to one regular cup as commonly served at Singaporean bubble tea and beverage stalls.

nutrient_source.title: Lemon Green Tea (No Topping) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Lemon Green Tea (No Topping), CR ID D08010065. All per-100g values are as reported.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2008 — sugar-sweetened tea and lemon tea analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for lemon green tea (no topping) as sold in Singapore. GI estimated at 54 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sugar-sweetened tea beverages and lemon-flavoured tea analogues (GI range 47–60 for similar sugar-sweetened tea drinks). Glycemic load per 250 g serving = (54 × 7.49 × 2.5) / 100 = 10.1 g. Estimation is conservative and consistent with the sugar content of 7.49 g/100 g. GI 54 is classified as low GI (55 or less).

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar content 7.49 g/100 g exceeds the 5 g/100 g SSB threshold; sugar-sweetened beverages independently raise blood pressure risk (AHA 2018) despite very low sodium of 1 mg/100 g.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request zero-sugar or unsweetened lemon green tea, as the added sugar content — not sodium — is the primary cardiovascular risk driver in this beverage.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 7.49 g sugar/100 g and zero dietary fibre; liquid sugar is rapidly absorbed, causing acute postprandial glycaemic excursions consistent with ADA SSB hard gate.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this beverage in its standard sweetened form and instead request unsweetened lemon green tea with no added sugar, as even a 250 ml serving delivers approximately 18.7 g of rapidly absorbed sugar.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages (7.49 g sugar/100 g) is associated with elevated triglycerides, increased CVD risk, and weight gain; the green tea polyphenol (EGCG) benefit is negated by the added sugar load.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should choose the unsweetened version of this drink to benefit from green tea catechins (EGCG) without the adverse metabolic effects of added sugar.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar 7.49 g/100 g with zero fibre promotes pro-inflammatory mediators, negating the anti-inflammatory EGCG polyphenol benefit of the green tea base.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should request unsweetened lemon green tea to obtain genuine anti-inflammatory benefit from EGCG and lemon vitamin C without the inflammatory burden of added sugar.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lemon green tea (no topping) is a popular cold or iced beverage widely sold at Singaporean bubble tea shops, hawker centres, and beverage stalls. It is made by brewing green tea leaves or green tea concentrate, then combining the brewed tea with lemon juice or fresh lemon slices and a measured quantity of sugar syrup, served chilled or over ice without any additional toppings such as pearls, jelly, or pudding. The drink has a light, refreshing, slightly astringent flavour from the green tea, balanced by the tartness of lemon and sweetness from added sugar. Per 100 g, it provides 29 kcal, 7.49 g carbohydrate (all as sugar), 0.01 g protein, 0 g fat, 0 g dietary fibre, and 1 mg sodium. Despite its low calorie and very low sodium profile, the 7.49 g/100 g added sugar content classifies it as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB), making it unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease in its standard sweetened form. The green tea base contains catechins including EGCG, which have documented anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, but these benefits are substantially offset by the added sugar. Requesting the unsweetened version is strongly recommended for health-conscious consumers. The beverage is vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID D08010065) with confidence 0.95 as reported in the input record. The source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore foods. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database, which reduces GI confidence to medium but does not affect overall record confidence given the strong nutrient provenance.
notes: fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo (D08010065); this is a sugar-sweetened tea beverage with no fat-containing ingredients. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero by the same source and formulation. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source; brewed tea with lemon juice contains no measurable dietary fibre. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero as this is a plant-based beverage with no animal-derived ingredients. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid profiles for this specific lemon green tea preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry D08010065 as of 2026. GI value of 54 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for sugar-sweetened tea beverages; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found. Glycemic load per 250 g serving calculated as (54 × 7.49 × 2.5) / 100 = 10.1 g. GI 54 is classified as low GI (55 or less).
